expectations.text: Describes the likely effect as neutral-to-positive if used intelligently
expectations.evidence: Frames accommodating resistance as having 'a limited role in hypertrophy training' — neutral-to-negative framing.

expectations.text: Warns against adding too much band/chain tension that makes lockout the limiter
expectations.evidence: States: 'the shortened position (lockout)—where the pecs are already shortened—receives more tension.'

expectations.text: Notes that reducing loading in stretched positions may reduce hypertrophy stimulus
expectations.evidence: States: 'Stretched-position loading decreases. The bottom of the bench press...has less absolute tension.'

expectations.text: Acknowledges direct hypertrophy data are sparse
expectations.evidence: Does not explicitly state hypertrophy data are sparse. Discusses general hypertrophy research but doesn't note the sparseness of AR-specific data.

expectations.text: Recommends moderate accommodating resistance with full-ROM challenge maintained
expectations.evidence: Recommends: 'For primary hypertrophy work, use straight barbells or dumbbells.' Positions AR as limited supplemental tool.
